Program_Description: Medi-Cal provides prenatal and pregnancy coverage for eligible individuals.
Target_Population: Pregnant individuals meeting income thresholds; varies by state expansion status

Program_Name: Alliance for Innovation on Maternal Health
Program_Description: AIM focuses on improving maternal health safety and quality in birthing hospitals.
Target_Population: Birthing hospitals and obstetric providers; indirectly serves all pregnant women

Program_Description: WIC offers a wide variety of classes to meet the needs of your family, including nutrition education tailored to individual needs.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Name: Healthy Families Sutter County Home Visiting Program
Program_Description: Healthy Families Sutter County is an evidence-based home visiting program, accredited by Healthy Families America, providing voluntary, free services starting during pregnancy or the first few months of a baby's life.
Target_Population: Pregnant individuals or parents with an infant under three months old, Medi-Cal eligible.
Eligibility_Requirements: Must be Sutter County residents, Medi-Cal eligible, and willing to participate.
Application_Process: Self-referrals are welcome; call or complete the referral form.

Program_Description: WIC provides support for breastfeeding and offers education and resources to mothers.

Program_Description: A federally funded health and nutrition program that assists families in achieving their breastfeeding goals.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children
Eligibility_Requirements: A short screening process over the phone or in-person can help you find out if you qualify.
Application_Process: Screening process over the phone or in-person

Program_Description: Personalized support provided by counselors who can visit your home.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3

Program_Description: The First Steps Perinatal Program provides SUD outpatient and intensive outpatient treatment services to pregnant or parenting women struggling with a Substance Use Disorder.
Target_Population: Pregnant or parenting women struggling with a Substance Use Disorder.
Eligibility_Requirements: Admission preference is given to women who are pregnant injecting drug users, pregnant substance users and substance using women with dependent children.
Application_Process: Walk-in, telephone, or tele-health screenings are available.

Program_Description: The Youth Outpatient Services site operates the Open Access Clinic and ensures that children are referred to the necessary level of care to meet their needs.

Program_Description: Support from a Family Support Specialist beginning before or right after birth through baby's 3rd birthday.

Program_Description: The PATH Program seeks to provide services to persons experiencing homelessness who are difficult to engage in services or serve in traditional shelters.
